description:
Configuration-management simulation: tracking a simple “package” change
------------------------------------------------------------------------

Background  
A small in-house configuration manager records the exact package list before and after **every** change.  
Your job is to simulate the addition of the package “jq 1.6” to an already-installed system and produce the diff the manager stores.

Required end-state of the system (everything **under /home/user**, no sudo needed)

1. Directory  
   /home/user/cfgmgr

2. Baseline file – *before* the change  
   Path: /home/user/cfgmgr/installed_packages_before.txt  
   Exact textual content (two lines, Unix line-endings, trailing newline **after each line**):  
   curl 7.88.1  
   git 2.39.2  

3. New state file – *after* the change  
   Path: /home/user/cfgmgr/installed_packages_after.txt  
   Exact textual content (three lines, Unix line-endings, trailing newline **after each line**):  
   curl 7.88.1  
   git 2.39.2  
   jq 1.6  

4. Unified diff patch  
   Path: /home/user/cfgmgr/jq_install_diff.patch  
   It must be the literal output produced by running  
   diff -u --label BEFORE --label AFTER installed_packages_before.txt installed_packages_after.txt  
   from inside /home/user/cfgmgr.  
   The file must therefore contain **exactly five lines** (each ending with “\n”) and look precisely like this:

   --- BEFORE  
   +++ AFTER  
   @@ -1,2 +1,3 @@  
    curl 7.88.1  
    git 2.39.2  
   +jq 1.6  

   Notes on verification:  
   • No timestamps or extra whitespace are allowed.  
   • Lines beginning with a single space must really start with one literal space.  
   • The “+jq 1.6” line starts with a plus sign immediately followed by “jq”.

High-level steps you will have to perform (for your reference only — do NOT print these as shell commands):
• Create the directory.  
• Create the baseline file with the two lines shown above.  
• Create the “after” file containing the baseline plus the new jq line.  
• Run the diff command with the two explicit labels and redirect its output exactly to jq_install_diff.patch.

Only those three files inside /home/user/cfgmgr will be checked by the automated grader.  
If any of them is missing, named differently, has extra blank lines, or deviates from the specified text, the task will be marked as failed.
truth:
Expected artefacts and their authoritative contents (all lines terminate with '\n'):

1. /home/user/cfgmgr/installed_packages_before.txt
---------------------------------------------------
curl 7.88.1
git 2.39.2

2. /home/user/cfgmgr/installed_packages_after.txt
--------------------------------------------------
curl 7.88.1
git 2.39.2
jq 1.6

3. /home/user/cfgmgr/jq_install_diff.patch
-------------------------------------------
--- BEFORE
+++ AFTER
@@ -1,2 +1,3 @@
 curl 7.88.1
 git 2.39.2
+jq 1.6

No additional files should be created in /home/user/cfgmgr.
